trivia: Airbus's carbon-fiber flagship: over half the airframe is composite, and its Trent XWB is often called the most efficient large turbofan flying. It owns many of the world's longest routes.
The curved black cockpit mask gives every A350 a raccoon face you can identify from the terminal in a second.
funFacts: Flies ultra-long-haul routes over 15,000 km nonstop.
53% of the structure is carbon-fiber composite.
